questions.q: Which empire did Chandragupta Maurya found?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Mauryan Empire around 321 BCE, India's first great pan-subcontinental state.

questions.q: Which dynasty did Chandragupta Maurya overthrow to seize power in Magadha?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta overthrew the Nanda dynasty that had ruled Magadha to establish Mauryan power.

questions.q: Which famous scholar and strategist is traditionally said to have guided Chandragupta to the throne?
questions.explanation: Chanakya, also known as Kautilya, is traditionally credited as the mentor who guided Chandragupta's rise.

questions.q: Approximately when did Chandragupta Maurya found his empire?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta founded the Mauryan Empire around 321 BCE.

questions.q: Which religion did Chandragupta Maurya reportedly embrace late in his life?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Chandragupta abdicated and embraced Jainism, ending his life as an ascetic.

questions.q: From which capital city was Chandragupta's empire governed?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra, near modern Patna, was the capital of the Mauryan Empire.

questions.q: Whose successors in the northwest did Chandragupta push back?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta drove back the successors of Alexander of Macedon from the northwestern regions of the subcontinent.

questions.q: Who succeeded Chandragupta Maurya as ruler of the Mauryan Empire?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta was succeeded by his son Bindusara in the early 3rd century BCE.

questions.q: What is Chandragupta Maurya best known for achieving in Indian history?
options: Unifying much of the subcontinent under one rule
questions.explanation: Chandragupta unified much of the Indian subcontinent under the first great Indian empire.

questions.q: Chandragupta Maurya's empire was centred on which historic kingdom of the Ganges plain?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan state grew out of Magadha, the kingdom of the Ganges plain in present-day Bihar.

questions.q: Which Seleucid ruler did Chandragupta Maurya conclude a treaty with around 305 BCE?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta defeated and then made a treaty with Seleucus I Nicator, one of Alexander's generals.

questions.q: According to the treaty with Seleucus, what did Chandragupta reportedly give in exchange for territory?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta is said to have given Seleucus 500 war elephants in exchange for territories in the northwest.

questions.q: Which Greek ambassador was sent by Seleucus to Chandragupta's court?
questions.explanation: Megasthenes was the Seleucid envoy at Pataliputra and wrote an account of India called the Indica.

questions.q: By what Greek name was Chandragupta Maurya known in classical Western sources?
questions.explanation: Greek and Roman sources refer to Chandragupta as Sandrocottus, a key clue for dating ancient Indian history.

questions.q: According to Jain tradition, where did Chandragupta spend his final ascetic days?
questions.explanation: Jain tradition holds that Chandragupta died as an ascetic at Shravanabelagola in present-day Karnataka.

questions.q: Which Jain saint did Chandragupta reportedly follow to the south at the end of his life?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Chandragupta accompanied the Jain teacher Bhadrabahu southward and practised ritual fasting unto death.

questions.q: Which Sanskrit play by Vishakhadatta dramatizes Chandragupta's rise and Chanakya's intrigues?
questions.explanation: The Mudrarakshasa by Vishakhadatta dramatizes the political plotting that brought Chandragupta to power.

questions.q: Which northwestern Punjab city was a major centre of learning associated with the early Mauryan northwest?
questions.explanation: Taxila in the northwest was a renowned centre of learning and is linked in tradition to Chandragupta and Chanakya.

questions.q: Megasthenes' account of Mauryan India was contained in a work titled what?
questions.explanation: Megasthenes recorded his observations of Chandragupta's empire in a work called the Indica, now surviving only in fragments.

questions.q: Which earlier conqueror's incursion into the northwest had left a power vacuum that aided Chandragupta's expansion there?
questions.explanation: Alexander's withdrawal from the northwest around 325 BCE left a vacuum that Chandragupta exploited.

questions.q: What was the family name or dynastic name derived from Chandragupta that the empire bore?
questions.explanation: The dynasty took the name Maurya from Chandragupta, giving the Mauryan Empire its name.

questions.q: Roughly how long did Chandragupta Maurya reign before his abdication?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta is generally held to have reigned about 24 years, roughly 321 to 297 BCE, before abdicating.

questions.q: Buddhist and some classical traditions claim Chandragupta belonged to which clan?
questions.explanation: Buddhist tradition links Chandragupta to the Moriya clan, one explanation offered for the Maurya name.

questions.q: The Junagadh rock inscription credits a Mauryan governor named Pushyagupta with building a dam on which lake during Chandragupta's reign?
questions.explanation: Rudradaman's later inscription records that Chandragupta's governor Pushyagupta built the Sudarshana Lake's dam in Saurashtra.

questions.q: The synchronism between Chandragupta and which Greek-attested figure provides the 'sheet anchor' for dating ancient Indian chronology?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta's contact with Alexander's successors gives historians the firm 'sheet anchor' date for Indian chronology.

questions.q: Which Greek historian's lost work, partly preserved by Justin, describes Chandragupta (Sandrocottus) raising an army against the Nandas?
questions.explanation: Pompeius Trogus's history, summarized by Justin, recounts Sandrocottus gathering forces and overthrowing the existing power.

questions.q: Which queen of Seleucus, according to some accounts, may relate to the marriage-alliance terms of the 305 BCE treaty (an 'epigamia')?
options: A marital alliance (epigamia) between the courts
questions.explanation: Classical sources mention an 'epigamia', or marriage relationship, agreed between the Mauryan and Seleucid houses.

questions.q: Which northwestern satrapies did Chandragupta gain from Seleucus, roughly corresponding to parts of modern Afghanistan and Baluchistan?
questions.explanation: The treaty ceded the satrapies of Arachosia, Gedrosia, Paropamisadae and part of Aria to Chandragupta.

questions.q: Which Tamil Sangam-era and later tradition, alongside Jain sources, references a southern connection for Chandragupta's final years?
options: Jain inscriptions and traditions at Shravanabelagola
questions.explanation: Jain inscriptions and tradition at Shravanabelagola preserve the account of Chandragupta's ascetic death in the south.

questions.q: Which classical author wrote that Sandrocottus possessed an army of 600,000 men?
questions.explanation: Pliny the Elder, drawing on earlier sources, recorded the figure of 600,000 for Chandragupta's standing army.
